extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won a reduced bunch sprint after breakaway caught at 5km, surviving late crash chaos that eliminated Gaviria and others.
tactical_patterns: Caja Rural attempted to thin peloton for their sprinter on rolling terrain 30km out, sprinters briefly distanced but rejoined.
Milan extended Ciclamino lead by contesting intermediate sprint against Pedersen despite being in breakaway, tactical points hoarding.
Ackermann won with minimal team support (only Gibbons helping), reveals UAE sprinter can execute without full leadout train.

rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: positive: contested intermediate sprint from breakaway AND nearly won bunch sprint, exceptional dual effort over 219km
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: first Giro stage win since 2019 with virtually no team, form resurging after lean years
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: suffering with illness again, unable to contest sprint on flat stage suited to him
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: negative: crash exit ends GC bid, Ineos now relying solely on Thomas without climber support
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: third place finish shows competitive sprint form despite age, regularly reaching podium positions
